reviews.review_title: I loved this on my Gear...
reviews.review_description: .... please port to quest, this makes for a good free example of VR.
One of my favourite apps on my GO, and one I use to impress my non VR friends. It's a shame that' it's not available on the Quest 2 though. Upgraded recently and disappointed it wasn't on the store 😢
This is a very good VR experience. Probably one of the most smooth and emersive experience I have ever tried! Please upgrade it to Quest!!

Can you upgrade this app for Oculus Quest? 
Thanks
Great graphics on Oculus Go, now i want AAA Mobile Full Game!
Among my favorite apps so far. I hope there are many more experiences in the Blade Runner universe planned. I keep going back to this. Make sure you’re seated in a swivel chair.
A great ride!
"so how good is this gear VR?" asks my friends and family. then i show them this. they are always "Whaaaaat?. this should be as official gvr demo

So detailed and smooth but no interaction.
I've just wanna more movies have the same high level vr-promos.
I found nothing to play, interact with, or stimulating about this download.
When your family turn this colour, ask them to take the headset off. Pretty cool for what it is but some of the camera movement was clearly done by someone sitting at a monitor.

Also would be nice to be able to fly where you want and land walk around.
I have this demo on Oculus go, so not really sure what you can do in the game.
Wow great experience!  Thanks!

One thing I’ve noticed in the six months I’ve owned my Oculus Go is that there seems to be a common theme with movie tie-ins and promos. Basically, developers have created short mini-games or mini-experiences free of charge as a means of promoting an upcoming film. Sounds like a brilliant marketing idea, right? Maybe it is, maybe it isn’t. That’s for the studio’s research team to decipher. Regardless, I feel like it is also an odd one. Quite a few of these titles are pushing VR to its limits in creative ways, emphasizing aspects of the film that are intended to draw in movie-goers. What we are getting are some pretty stunning VR demos, brilliant in either the graphics or in gameplay. And they are free, so that’s a huge win. But imagine seeing only one scene of a brilliant movie and never getting to view the whole film. Or reading only part of a classic novel and never getting to experience the whole thing. This is how we’re left to feel with these mini experiences.

Blade Runner 2049: Replicant Pursuit is, in a word, incredible. The moment I entered this world, I was speechless. Once I finally lifted my jaw off the floor, I just soaked in this experience. When it ended, I was absolutely flabbergasted. How was this not a full-length A-list title at a premium price? If the game were expanded to a proper length and included a feature set that justified the cost, I doubt anyone would regret the purchase. Seems like the developers have left a ton of cash on the table here. I felt the same about Tomb Raider VR, and to a slightly lesser extent Suicide Squad, that I wonder to what degree these VR studios are aware of the missed opportunities. Having said all of that, you will be disappointed only with Blade Runner’s duration. Even if it is merely a commercial for the film in VR, it is still an elite one, and one that highlights just how superior it is to the vast majority of product on the market.
This was really cool.  Wish it were longer.

I’m new to VR, but this is great entertainment, and there should be more ‘rides’ just like it. It’s well scripted, graphics are really nice and it’s very immersive. Will be on my Go for a long time to come.

This is a pretty good VR experience and like others it seems a little short though. One thing that would be nice to if they could put the pilot in the drivers seat and let us see through his eyes. Instead of seeing a empty seat,  just a thought! But otherwise good job developers...
Managed to get this working well worth a look, realy good realism and gameplay. Looking forward to full game.
Not need to go to Universal Studio with Oculus go.  A really cool ride free of charge and no waiting in line for an hour.  GREAT
Wish it were longer, very cool immersive experience!
